cbtTechniques.name: Thought Challenging / Cognitive Restructuring
cbtTechniques.description: Identifying and disputing irrational or maladaptive thoughts to replace them with more balanced alternatives.
examples: Using a thought record to analyze the evidence for and against a negative belief.
Recognizing a cognitive distortion and actively writing down a more realistic thought.
reframingQuestions: What is the evidence that supports or contradicts my current thought?
Is there a more helpful or realistic way to view this situation?

cbtTechniques.name: Behavioral Activation
cbtTechniques.description: Scheduling and engaging in positive, meaningful, or highly rewarding activities to combat depressive symptoms and lethargy.
examples: Committing to calling a friend even when feeling unmotivated.
reframingQuestions: How might taking a small, positive action right now shift my mood?
What is one small activity I used to enjoy that I can try for 10 minutes today?

cbtTechniques.name: Exposure Techniques
cbtTechniques.description: Gradually and systematically facing feared objects, situations, or memories in a safe environment to reduce avoidance and anxiety.
examples: Looking at pictures of spiders before progressing to being in the same room as one.
Practicing public speaking in front of a mirror before speaking to a small group.
reframingQuestions: What is the smallest, safest step I can take toward facing this fear?
If the worst-case scenario happened during this exposure, how would I handle it?

cbtTechniques.name: Relaxation and Mindfulness
cbtTechniques.description: Using physiological techniques and present-moment awareness to reduce physical arousal and psychological distress.
examples: Practicing progressive muscle relaxation before bed.
Doing a 5-minute deep breathing exercise to center oneself during a stressful workday.
reframingQuestions: Where am I holding physical tension in my body right now?
Can I gently bring my attention back to the rhythm of my breath?

cbtTechniques.name: Problem-Solving Therapy
cbtTechniques.description: A structured method for identifying problems, brainstorming solutions, and implementing action plans.
examples: Breaking down a massive financial debt into small, actionable monthly budgeting steps.
Creating a pros-and-cons list to decide whether to change careers.
reframingQuestions: What is the specific, solvable problem I am facing right now?
What are three possible solutions, regardless of how silly they might seem at first?
